title: iPhone 17 Pro 60 Days Later! I've Changed My Mind
transcript: So, I've been using the iPhone 17 Pro Max for quite some time now, and these are my honest thoughts about this phone after using it for about 60 days. First up, the design this year feels better on paper. I mean, the aluminum unibody should in theory make it a little lighter. The new back camera layout is refreshing after so many years, although it's still quite similar to the old one. But in reality, these changes make little to no difference at all. In fact, the aluminum unibody design is more prone to scratches, and this phone is almost as heavy as last year's iPhone 16 Pro Max thanks to its bigger battery. Overall, the design isn't that big of an upgrade when compared to last year's model. If you're planning to get the 17 Pro models just because of the design, then you might be disappointed. The orange iPhone for so many people is losing its color and turning pink. Apple has officially issued a statement on this and they're saying not to use any kind of liquid product while cleaning the orange iPhone 17 Pro model. PWM dimming technology is helping by putting less strain on the eyes. If you have an iPhone 17, then I highly recommend turning on this one option for a better user experience. The display is superb this year as we are getting a much brighter panel that can go up to 3000 nits of peak brightness. Since I'm using a screen protector on my iPhone, I wasn't able to test the new anti-reflective coating on these iPhones. For me, the safety of the display comes first. For the safety of that aluminum unibody design, I'm using a clear case from ESR. I have some other cases too from Pitaka that go pretty well with my iPhone 17 Pro Max in silver color. I face frequent Wi-Fi disconnections with iOS 26 on the iPhone 17 Pro Max. I updated it to iOS 26.1 and those issues were still there. Now I'm on iOS 26.2 2 beta 2 and things are a bit better, but I'm hoping for even more improvements with upcoming iOS versions. Other than these few things, this phone is a monster. The performance is superb, although there are a few hiccups when scrolling across the UI. Once again, thanks to iOS 26, but overall, this phone has better heat management and the battery life is amazing. If you're not a super heavy user, then this phone will easily last for two days. Cameras are about the same as the 16 Pro Max, but things got a little better here, especially with the telephoto camera, and overall processing is better, too, this year. By the way, that camera control button is still kind of useless for me. I'm not using it at all for 2 years in a row at this point. So, in short, after using the iPhone 17 Pro Max for almost 2 months, it is definitely an amazing phone, but with a few downsides and hiccups that are due to the new aluminum uni-body design and iOS 26. If you have an iPhone 14 or earlier, upgrading to this phone would be ideal. Let me know your thoughts in the comment section and tell me which phone you're using right now. With that said, I'll catch you in the next one.

title: iPhone 17 Pro nach 2 Monaten! - Lohnt es sich wirklich? (review)
transcript: This is the iPhone 17 Pro, Apple's latest flagship model.  Not only does it have a new design, but also a new camera and a larger battery.  But is it all really as wonderful as it sounds?  Or is there a catch somewhere? A big thank you goes first and foremost to Notebooksbilliger.de, who provided me with the iPhone 17 Pro and without whom this review would not have been possible .  So be sure to check out notebooksbilliger.de next time you're buying tech.  Design: I don't think there has ever been an iPhone model that was as striking at first glance as the iPhone 17 Pro.  Well , at least if you, like me, opt for the new bright orange color , which I find incredibly daunting, by the way .  If you don't want that, you can choose between a dark blue and a silver.  And that was it .  Yes, really.  There is no other color available, and in my opinion, they could have offered a black version and perhaps a natural titanium version like the iPhone 16 Pro, because as it is, the selection is very limited.  Okay, but now let's move away from the colors and towards the design, which is new and takes some getting used to.  If you ask me how I would describe it, I would clearly say resilient and massive. I wouldn't even use the word "premium" in the case of the iPhone 17 Pro, even though it looks really high-quality in its own way.  The very latest change, however, is the casing of the iPhone 17 Pro, as it is now made of aluminum and glass.  More precisely, the aluminum frame flows onto the back and seamlessly forms the camera element.  There is relatively little glass left.  However, it can be found on the back in the form of a rectangular area, which is primarily there to ensure that MaxSaf and wireless charging still work without problems .  The advantage of using so much aluminum is, on the one hand, that it can no longer break, but on the other hand, aluminum attracts far more scratches, so there are already countless reports that the iPhone 17 Pro case attracts more scratches than anyone had hoped. Well, even though the iPhone 17 Pro is a bit thicker than its predecessor , it still feels just as good and soft in the hand, which I really like .  But let's now talk about the camera element that was briefly mentioned earlier.  It extends across the entire width of the back, and even though you might think that the three built-in cameras, which are still arranged as in the predecessor, are therefore completely recessed in the camera element, I unfortunately have to disappoint you and say: "No, the lenses still protrude individually and are therefore not only not well protected, but the whole thing also looks really strange."  Okay, so all that's missing now are the stereo speakers and water resistance.  Let's start with the speakers.  It is rare to hear such a clear, rich sound from a smartphone, and even though some competing smartphones are getting closer to it , they often lack that final touch of quality, which the speakers of the 17 Pro possess.  Especially since they would be even better compared to the iPhone 16 Pro .  I honestly don't know of any other smartphone that has better features, and I think that will remain the case for a very long time .  The iPhone is of course waterproof again, and just like with the speakers, it's once again number 1 in the entire smartphone market.  Because it can withstand water levels up to 6 meters deep for 30 minutes.  Display.  As was already the case with the predecessor and the predecessor of the predecessor, there are two different versions.  The iPhone 17 Pro and the iPhone 17 Pro Max. This means one has a 6.3-inch display and the other a 6.9-inch display.  I myself have the regular iPhone 17 Pro, and therefore from now on we will only be talking about that one.  As expected, you get OLED 120 Hz, a PPI of 460, and a maximum brightness of 3000 nits, if you ask me.  A really beautiful display that you always enjoy looking at.   The thin display bezels further enhance the effect, giving the impression that you are only holding the display in your hand.  Oh, and I almost forgot, there's a new display glass called Ceramic Shield 2. It's not only more scratch-resistant, but also reflects less light, which I really appreciate.  However, there aren't quite as few reflections as, for example, on the Samsung Galaxy S25 Ultra. But it is less than its predecessor.  The truly stylish display is only interrupted by the Dynamic Island, which can display some additional information. However, I personally notice time and again that I hardly use Dynamic Island and that there are still only a few functions that are truly useful, and only sporadically .  I mean, sure, when listening to music it 's kind of handy to be able to change the song using the dynamic island, for example, but I don't really use this function for much more than that .  Importantly, Face ID is of course still on board, with the necessary sensors located in the dynamic island, ensuring that what I believe is the most intuitive unlocking method has once again found its way into the iPhone 17 Pro .  Unlocking works as always, totally intuitive, and I am and will remain a big fan of Face ID performance. Inside the iPhone 17 Pro, of course, is Apple's latest Pro chip , the A19 Pro Bionic chip, which is supported by 12 GB of RAM. In this review, I'll try to avoid bothering you with numbers as much as possible and instead describe everything in a more roundabout way. As far as the CPU of the A19 Pro is concerned, you get one of the most powerful currently on the market, and the GPU is also top-of-the- line, almost twice as powerful as the A18 Pro.  The iPhone 17 Pro even features a new, innovative cooling technology.   To achieve this, Apple developed a laser-welded vapor chamber that works in conjunction with the aluminum unibody structure to direct heat away from the A19 Pro chip. But what does all this tell us?  Well, to put it simply, the iPhone 17 Pro once again boasts brilliant performance, and the cooling technology further ensures consistently good performance. In everyday use, it's therefore nothing special that everything runs smoothly without the slightest bugs or similar issues, and that apps open quickly and stay open in the background for a very long time. Software.  As is customary for a new iPhone model, the iPhone 17 Pro also receives the latest iOS version, namely iOS 26. It should have been clear to everyone from the start that this version did n't exactly receive universally positive feedback, because with iOS 26 and the new Liquid Glass design, everything about the software has become, as the name suggests, more glass-like, playful, and transparent. Many elements now really look and behave like glass or liquid glass .  Personally, I have to say that I often really like it and it somehow fits in with the iPhones.  However, not everything is perfect, and there are still occasional glitches, display errors, and elements that are really difficult to see or read in some situations. Apple needs to adjust or optimize a few things here as quickly as possible .  But if that happens, and I'm sure it will, and faster than we think, then I really have to say that I 'm a fan of iOS 26.  Even though it is, of course, a very drastic update.  What continues to disappoint me, however, is and remains Apple Intelligence.  While some things have changed, I especially like the new Circle to Search.   But we still have to wait, for example, for Siri to finally become more intelligent .  What Google and Samsung have been achieving for some time now is far more impressive.  Battery.  Yes, I actually have some very good news here, because the battery has gotten bigger.  More precisely, the iPhone 17 Pro saw an 11% increase.  Accordingly, I naturally got through a day without any problems using the iPhone 17 Pro.  In the evenings, I consistently had around 50% battery left, even after 4 to 5 hours of screen usage , which means that in many cases you can easily get 6 or maybe even 7 hours of screen time.  Of course, it always depends on how much mobile data and Wi-Fi you use while traveling. The iPhone delivers very good battery life again this year. However, it must also be said that there are a few other smartphones that now offer significantly better battery life.  Charging is done at 40 watts, so you can reach 50% within 20 minutes.  This is a very good development for Apple.  G2 Wireless Charging and MaxSaf with 25 watts are also included, of course.  Camera.  That's also the last point I'd like to discuss with you.  This year, less has changed than last year , but at least all three built-in cameras are now equipped with 48 megapixels, as the telephoto camera has also caught up, meaning there is now a 48 megapixel main camera, a 48 megapixel ultra-wide-angle camera, and the 48 megapixel telephoto camera with 4x optical zoom.  But what are the results?  Well, first of all, the ultra-wide-angle camera naturally has a macro mode again.  It's good, and the close focusing distance has also been improved.   In terms of color, the images are generally very convincing, and the dynamic range and sharpness are also simply top-notch.  I also like the wide field of view of this ultra-wide-angle lens.  I'm really pleased with the results from the main camera again .  They are beautiful with accurate colors, very high dynamic range and crisp sharpness.  Yes, I like the skin tones too , but it must be said that a OnePlus 13 or Google Pixel 10 Pro delivers even better ones.  Incidentally, you can take photos in 24 or 48 megapixels, and if you want the best quality, you should definitely shoot in RAW.  Everything I've just said about the main camera and the ultra-wide-angle lens can actually be applied equally to the telephoto camera.  It now has a four-times optical zoom, which I really like.  I really like the focal length and therefore I love taking photos with this camera , especially because the results are very detailed.  In conclusion, regarding the iPhone 17 Pro camera, it can definitely be said that you get another very good one, but there are now other and better cameras available, and Apple has stagnated a little in this area in recent years , as not much has really changed and other manufacturers have overtaken them in camera technology here and there .  Okay, now that we've cleared all that up , we really only need to talk about the videos , because they are still the best on a smartphone.  You can record again in 4K at up to 120 FPS and of course, if you want, also in Pro Look.  Naturally, I also find the videos visually incredibly appealing.  The image stabilization is also excellent.  Oh, and since this year it's even possible to record videos with Openate or Apple Log.  I also think the new square front camera is cool, which allows you to hold the iPhone normally and still take landscape photos .  Conclusion: While using the iPhone 17 Pro , I kept wondering if I was simply using an iPhone 16 Pro with a different design and a starting price of €1299.  And ultimately, that's pretty much the case, with the exception that a few things have been tweaked here and there, which is why the iPhone 17 Pro is not really spectacular, but still a very good smartphone with only a few significant weaknesses, and I think that describes it quite well.   So, especially if you have a lot of Apple products, or if your next smartphone is going to be an iPhone, or another iPhone, then the iPhone 17 Pro is actually a device that you can't really go wrong with. Yes.

title: Ringke Fusion iPhone 17 Pro Max Review - A $16 Case That Performs Way Above Its Price

My scoring system is based on what the iPhone actually feels when it hits the ground on its corner, on its face, as well as on its camera lump. Each case gets dropped 30 times. So, when I say it's terrible, it's because I've got the data to back it up. In terms of design, this is the floor for a clear case, TPU wedges, polycarbonate back. This thing's going to be a fingerprint magnet for darker iPhones, but Ricky has added a few quality of life features. Raised corners for screen access as well as protection. There's little feet on the bottom of the case so that it's not scratching and prevents sliding around. Terms of weight, this thing weighs 36 g, rivaling some of Apple's own cases. When it comes to bulk, it's only going to increase it by 58%. There isn't much to this product. Now, when it comes to handling, the case actually is super slick despite having a TPU edge, which is weird. It feels very cheap hand feel-wise. Buttons are integrated into the edge of the case. There's a cutout for the camera control button, and the port cutout feels a little rough on the backside, so pinky propping it up is uh pretty terrible with this product. Now, screen access for this product is awesome because the edges are super low, but remember, the corners are bigger than average, so you don't have to worry about uh screen clearance. When it came to a mag safe accessories to pull things off only took 8.6 newtons to push things off took 8.9 newtons. That's not great. The only Mags safe accessory I worry about using it with this case is going to be a uh car mount. Like that's not good. Now when it comes to protective design for this product, the corners are only 1.4 wo millimeters thick. The back is only 2 mm thick. This case does have sizable air pockets for added protection, but the thickness of material between the air pocket and edge of the case is like less than a mil. It's crazy. Now, when it comes to rigidity, this product is uh pretty terrible because it only took 18.6 newtons to bend at 8 ms, which is 35% below average. Honestly, at this point, things were not looking good for this product. The only saving grace for this product is that it will not slide around very easily. No chance of it going off a table. When it came to corner drops, my iPhone only felt 9.2 gs, about 12% better than average. The rate of rotation after the corner drop was very average, and the settling rate was also very average. And the total force going to the iPhone was 70% better than average. Given how tiny this case is, these are really good drop numbers. But don't worry, there is a catch. When it came to face first drops, it passed. The initial impact was 36% better than average. settling rate was 30% better than average and the total force going into the iPhone was 11% better than average. title: iPhone 17 Pro Max nach 2 Monaten! Mein Fazit
transcript: The iPhone 17 Pro Max, arguably the most controversial iPhone of all time.  Many hate the new design, others say it 's the best update in years.  I used the device almost daily for two months and now I'll honestly tell you what's really good, what Apple completely messed up, and whether it's worth switching.  Apple has just received criticism that the iPhones look the same every year and that many find the familiar design boring [music].  Now Apple has dared to make a design change, and apparently many people are n't happy about it.  I personally see it differently.  The design is of course a matter of taste, but it definitely looks new and different.  Nevertheless, it retains the typical iPhone look, primarily due to the three cameras .  What I particularly like is that the design is also functional.  The new unibody aluminum frame dissipates heat from the phone many times better.  This, combined with the new steam chamber, has ensured that this iPhone has not become uncomfortably warm even once in the last two months .  However, the disadvantage of aluminum compared to the previous titanium is its lower hardness.  The titanium 5 used in the iPhone 16 Pro is twice as hard as the aluminum used in the iPhone 17 Pro. You see some really extreme cases on the internet that make you think the iPhone 17 Pro Max really has problems. But one must always be very careful with such reports, as they are mostly used for quick headlines and are not always true to reality.  According to YouTuber Cherry Erick Everything, who professionally scratches smartphones, the casing still has a very low susceptibility to scratches.  The only real weakness is the very sharp corners in the camera platform. These can scratch more easily because the colored coating doesn't adhere as well there .  If you like to use your iPhone without a case, I recommend the silver color, because scratches are hardly noticeable there .  I personally often use my orange iPhone 17 Pro Max at home without a case and haven't gotten any scratches on it yet .  It is clear that Apple is increasingly positioning the Pro models as a professional tool and less as a lifestyle accessory, as was the case with previous iPhone generations.  The iPhone Air is intended to be the slim and elegant model for design enthusiasts, while the Pro models are specifically designed for professional users and power users .  This focus is also reflected in the features that Apple has recently introduced specifically for the Pro models.  Therefore, some of these new Pro features, such as Pro RAW and Open Gate, offer no added value for the average user who only consumes social media, CarPlay and music on their smartphone in everyday life. Nevertheless, the iPhone 17 Pro Max also has some advantages for average users .  One of the biggest factors is battery life.  This one is truly phenomenal.  In my daily routine, I end my day with about 40% battery remaining after 4 hours of screen time.  It definitely has a bit more battery than the iPhone 16 Pro Max. I never felt that the battery life was insufficient.  The iPhone 17 Pro Max, up to 40 watts, can be charged wirelessly for approximately 50% of its battery life in 20 minutes, making it unmatched.  I usually charge my iPhone with a Maxsaf charger. It charges at 25 watts, which is quite fast for wireless charging. Next, I'd like to talk about the camera .  And the camera is very important to me personally, as I always want the highest quality photos and videos.  Unfortunately, there are very few smartphones that take both good pictures and good videos.  The iPhone 17 Pro Max is one of those smartphones.  However, this year we have a four-times zoom camera instead of a five-times zoom camera.  The telephoto camera now offers 48 megapixels.  This means you can zoom up to 1x and still have 12 megapixel image material.  The photos are really very good from all three lenses. Especially if, like me, you often take RAW images and then edit them in Lightroom, you get results that no longer look like they were taken with a smartphone camera.  Even without RAW mode, photos look very good in all lighting conditions.  Photos are saved at 24 megapixels by default .  Most Android smartphones have cameras with more megapixels and can store photos, but by default they still only save photos in 12 megapixels.  I mostly only use the camera button, which was also present in the iPhone 16 Pro, to quickly open the camera app and not really to zoom or open camera settings .  I can't find the right position in either landscape or portrait orientation to comfortably take photos with it .  Furthermore, the pictures often look blurry when I trigger a photo with the button. The new front camera is a really good camera update .  This one now has 18 megapixels.  The difference is most noticeable in a direct comparison, but it 's not a world of difference.  The real highlight is the new square sensor.  This means that you can take photos with the selfie camera in both portrait and landscape format without having to rotate the phone. In addition, the front camera has a wider focal length, which makes group selfies significantly better .  Another highlight of the iPhone 17 Pro Max is the display, because with a peak brightness of up to 3000 LZ I no longer have any problems seeing my screen content even in direct sunlight .  Thanks to improved thermal management, peak brightness now lasts considerably longer, even at higher temperatures. Apple has also given the iPhone an anti-reflective coating. Unfortunately, it's not as powerful as the one on the S25 Ultra.  You only notice the difference in a direct comparison with the iPhone 16 Pro, but in my opinion the best improvement to the display this year is Ceramic Shield 2. Initially, I thought the three times better scratch resistance that Apple advertises was just pure marketing.  But according to the scratch tests by Cherry Ricks Everything, the Ceramic Shield 2 display is significantly more resistant to scratches.  It is so scratch-resistant that you don't actually need a protective glass for normal use.  Some people get headaches when the screen brightness is low.  The reason?  Most smartphones dim the screen by quickly switching the light on and off.  This is called pulse-width modulation, or PWM for short.  [music] This rapid flickering is not visible to the naked eye, but can trigger headaches in sensitive individuals.  The iPhone 17 Pro Max now offers a solution.  Under Accessibility, Display and Text Size, you can turn off PWM.  The display is then dimmed in a different way, without the annoying flickering.  Speakers are an often underestimated category, and here the iPhone 17 Pro Max far surpasses the competition.  The sound from the speaker is louder, but also richer, and especially with lower tones you notice a big difference compared to all other smartphones.  The speakers are so good that I use them in the bathroom while showering to listen to podcasts or music .  As every year, Apple is leading the way with its processors, and this time too the A9 Pro chip is the most powerful chip on the market. Accordingly, performance was not even remotely a problem.  Let's just briefly discuss the iOS 26 software. Some love it, others ca n't stand the new style at all. The new liquid glass design is, of course, a matter of taste. I personally like the fish look and think it makes iOS look more modern.   What I found particularly positive was that the system as a whole runs much more smoothly and stably than at the start.  Many bugs were fixed with the update to iOS 26.1 .  Since then, I haven't had any supports or trailers .  Overall, I am really impressed with the iPhone 17 Pro Max.  It's not perfect, but it comes very close to meeting my needs.  Anyone who is currently considering buying a new iPhone will definitely make the right decision with the iPhone 17 Pro Max .   Feel free to write your opinion in the comments and comment where you are currently watching this video from.  from an iPhone or a Samsung.  I 'm excited and I'll give the video a thumbs up and subscribe, and then we'll see each other again in the next video.

title: iPhone 17 Pro vs iPhone 17: ¿Cuál deberías comprar?
transcript: Today we're going to compare the new iPhone 17 Pro with the standard 17, analyzing in detail their differences in design, performance, cameras, battery, screen, and exclusive features.  If you're thinking about upgrading your phone or deciding between one or another, this video will help you get a clear picture of which one is best for you to buy.  Let's start with the design.  At first glance, both models look very similar, but there are key differences.  The iPhone 17 Pro has a new aluminum structure with slightly more rounded edges.  There's less glass on the back, and that's a good decision because it makes the device more resistant to drops.  Although there is much controversy surrounding the color because it is more prone to scratches than previous versions.  To be honest, neither of mine got scratched, but this can happen if you don't use it with a case.  The camera module has been extended and now has relief.  The glass part has Max Safe. The entire back features the new vapor chamber that helps to better dissipate heat.  In contrast, the iPhone 17 uses recycled aluminum that feels lighter but less solid in the hand. The design is exactly the same as last year's 16.  Both are very comfortable in the hand thanks to their weight and dimensions, but the 17 seems better to me because it has flat edges.  The Proesa 206g and the 17 is lighter, specifically at 177g.  Thanks to this matte finish, they don't get as dirty if you use them without a case, but it's important to consider putting one on because they can get damaged or even faded if dropped.  We found no difference on the screens .  Both use 6.3-inch or Super Retina XDR LED panels with promotional technology, which means you can adjust the refresh rate from 1 to 120 Hz depending on the content.  This makes animations, scrolling, and gameplay feel much smoother. In addition, the maximum brightness reaches 3000 nits with the new anti-reflective coating and Cerx Shield 2 protection. Both feature the dynamic island at the top for Face ID and the front camera.  Both offer a great visual experience with very narrow bezels, excellent viewing angles on a flat panel that provides great ergonomics.  I like that Apple finally abandoned 60Hz and put a screen with a decent refresh rate in its version 17. Now let's talk about performance, one of the most anticipated aspects of each year.  The iPhone 17 Pro is on the A19 Pro chip, manufactured in a second-generation 3 nanm process.  The iPhone 17, meanwhile, integrates the A19 chip, which is still very powerful, but doesn't quite reach the level of the Pro. This translates to faster loading times for demanding apps, better performance in demanding games, and an overall smoother experience on the Pro model. Furthermore, the Pro features a six-core GPU and full hardware R-tracing support, enabling more realistic graphics in compatible games.  In summary, both offer good performance and you probably won't even notice the difference in everyday use.  The iPhone 17 is less powerful than the Pro, but if you need maximum power, the Pro is going to be the best option.  Let's move on to the cameras, where Apple makes differences between the models.  The iPhone 17 Pro has a triple camera system, a 48-megapixel sensor with f1.7 aperture, a 48-megapixel ultra-wide sensor, and a 48- megapixel periscope telephoto lens with up to four times optical zoom .  In contrast, the iPhone 17 features a dual 48-megapixel main camera and a 48-megapixel ultra-wide camera without a telephoto lens.  This means that the Pro offers more photographic versatility, especially for portraits and distant shots.  In addition, the image processing in the Pro takes advantage of the new chip and the new, improved photonic engine, achieving better dynamic range, more natural colors, and less noise in low light.  The iPhone 17 camera will be more than enough for many people's daily needs.  They're not the same as the Pro ones, but they do offer good results both day and night.  If you don't need the telephoto lens, the iPhone 17 will probably be an excellent camera for your everyday use.  In video, both record at 4K 60 frames per second, but the Pro adds support for 4K 120 frames per second recording, pre-rock gen lo log, and log 2 format. It also allows you to record directly to external drives via USB Type-C, something the base model lacks.  The front camera on both is exactly the same with a 12-megapixel resolution, a square sensor with greater framing flexibility, and the center stage function that automatically expands to include more people in group selfies or keeps you centered in video calls.  Another notable new feature is dual capture, which allows you to record videos simultaneously with the front camera and one of the rear cameras.  This is a video test with the front camera in 4K 30 frames per second with the iPhone 17 Pro and I find it much more comfortable to record this way because I have the phone in portrait mode, but I'm recording in landscape mode and it's definitely a huge improvement.  Speaking of the USBC port, both models finally adopt that standard, but again the Pro has the advantage.  Its port supports 3.2 transfer speeds, while the standard iPhone 17 only reaches 2.0 speeds equivalent to those of the old Lightning port.  Let's move on to batteries.  Apple promises improvements in both models thanks to the efficiency of the new chip and a slightly larger battery .  On the iPhone 17, the average duration is around 30 hours of video playback, while the 17 Pro reaches 33 hours.  It's not a huge difference, but it is noticeable in everyday life.  intensive.  Both are compatible with H2 fast and wireless charging.  As for the software, both come with iOS 26. The experience is very similar to previous versions. Apple introduced transparency in the aesthetic aspect, which I like, but we are still waiting for updates to Apple Intelligence, which to date has not been improved.  Another difference is in the memory of the iPhone 17 and Pro this year, which start from 256 GB of storage.  The Pro includes 12 GB of RAM compared to 8 GB in the standard model.  This contributes to better multitasking performance and greater device longevity.  In terms of audio, both maintain stereo speakers with good balance, but the Pro has a wider profile and greater clarity in the mid frequencies, especially when playing Dolby Atmos content.  I'll leave you with a sample now so you can get an idea of ​​how they both sound. As for prices, the iPhone 17 starts at $99, while the 17 Pro starts at $99.99.   That 's a 300 difference that's justified if you're really going to take advantage of the Pro's extra hardware, but it might not be worth it if your use is more casual.  In conclusion, in terms of overall user experience, the iPhone 17 offers virtually everything most users need.  Great on screen. Excellent main cameras, good performance, more than enough battery life , but the 17 Pro feels more focused on demanding users, content creators or those who value the best technology available in a phone.  If you're not interested in recording in PR, playing games at maximum quality, or professionally editing content , the standard model will do the job perfectly.  And that concludes this comparison of the iPhone 17 Pro i17.  Leave a comment telling us which one you would buy and why.  I'm interested to know what you think.  If you liked or found the video helpful, don't forget to leave a like, subscribe to the channel, and as always, thanks for watching.

title: iPhone 17/17 Pro/Pro Max: Fix Slow Keyboard Typing Issue After iOS 26
transcript: Hey, what's up guys? So, in this video, we will see how to fix the slow keyboard typing issue on the iPhone 17, 17 Pro, and the 17 Pro Max. Now, to do that, just open settings, then tap on general. From here, you will find the option of transfer or reset iPhone. Just tap on it. Then tap on reset. From this menu, just select reset keyboard dictionary. Then enter your lock screen pin and select reset dictionary. After that just go back to the main settings page. And from here just go to accessibility and in accessibility you will find the option of keyboards and typing. Just tap on it. Now at the bottom of the page you will find the option of slow keys. Just tap on it. Then disable slow keys. After you disable this option, then go back to the main settings page and tap on general. From here, just tap on keyboards. And on this page, you will have to disable two options. First of all, just disable autocorrection. And after that, just scroll down and disable predictive text. After you've done that, then go to your home screen and reboot your iPhone. For that, just press and release the volume up button and the volume down button. Now, just press and hold the side button on your iPhone until the screen of your iPhone goes black and the Apple logo appears on the screen. Once the Apple logo appears on the screen, just release the side button. Now, if you have done all that and if this did not fix your issue, then this could be a software glitch. For that, From here just tap on software update. Now just check if you have any software update available. If you have any software update available just update the software of your iPhone.
